title: Benchmark result evidence row
description: Reproducible benchmark measurement attached to a Gaia skill as an evidence row. Sprint D W2a (issue #904) — field names and shape are FROZEN post-merge (API contract for downstream benchmark indices W2b/W3/W4). Two provenance paths are honored: verifier-attested (4★+ verifier sign-off) or ci-reproduced (GitHub Actions workflow run on the submitted commit). Self-attested provenance is FOREVER rejected. Mirrored rows appear as citations only and are excluded from Trust Magnitude. This standalone sub-schema is citable from external clients (docs/benchmarks/methodology.md links here); enforcement inside the skill graph happens via the mirrored allOf clause on evidenceEntry in skill.schema.json.

type.description: Fixed discriminator. Matched by the evidenceEntry allOf/if branch to gate the benchmark-only required fields.

source.description: Public URL where the benchmark run or report can be inspected (leaderboard entry, blog post, CI workflow run, arXiv paper). Mirrors evidenceEntry.source.

evaluator.description: GitHub username of the person who filed the evidence row. For CI-reproduced rows this is typically the workflow owner or a bot handle.

date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the evidence was filed against the registry. Distinct from runAt (when the benchmark itself was run).

benchmarkId.description: Semver-ish identifier of the benchmark spec. Widened to accept humaneval@v1.0, humaneval/python@v1.0, swe-bench_verified@1.0, mmlu-5shot@2024-03. FROZEN — extensions require a major schema bump.

score.description: Raw score as reported by the harness, in the units named by `unit`. Interpretation is unit-specific: pass@1 is 0..1, pct is 0..100, elo is unbounded, etc.

unit.description: FROZEN enum of score units. Extending this list is a major schema bump — downstream leaderboard consumers switch on it.

runAt.description: ISO 8601 date-time (with timezone) the benchmark harness was executed. Feeds the freshness half-life calculation in Trust Magnitude.

provenance.description: How the row's authenticity is anchored. verifier-attested: a 4★+ Verifier co-signed the score. ci-reproduced: a GitHub Actions workflow ran the harness on a public commit SHA. mirrored: citation-only copy from an external leaderboard (excluded from Trust Magnitude — see trustMagnitude.py). pending: default state for freshly filed rows awaiting promotion; rejected on main by scripts/validate.py --strict. self-attested is intentionally absent and FOREVER rejected.

attestor.description: Human-readable attestor identity. For verifier-attested: GitHub username of the co-signing verifier. For ci-reproduced: workflow run URL plus commit SHA (e.g. 'https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/12345@abc1234'). For mirrored: upstream leaderboard URL. For pending: filer's own handle.

datasetHash.description: SHA-256 (64 lowercase hex chars) of the raw dataset used for evaluation. Locks the row to a specific dataset version — a dataset revision produces a different hash, and thus a different row.

benchmarkInputHash.description: SHA-256 (64 lowercase hex chars) of the concatenation of (dataset + prompt template + harness config). Reproducibility fingerprint — a downstream verifier can re-run the harness with the same inputs and expect the same score. Different concept from datasetHash: two rows may share datasetHash but differ in benchmarkInputHash if the harness config or prompt template changed.

percentile.description: Optional placement percentile (0..100) within the field of published scores on this benchmark. Feeds the benchmark-result magnitude formula in trustMagnitude.py (magnitude = percentile).

harnessUrl.description: Optional URL to the exact harness code used to produce the score. For CI-reproduced rows this is typically a permalink into scripts/benchmarks/<benchmark>/run.py at the pinned commit.
